Item II.

Does (1.3) hold for any function \(\alpha\) such that \(\alpha'\) \(a.e.\) exists and is in \(L^{1}[a, b]\text{?}\) More generally, under what conditions on a function \(f\) on \([a, b]\) there would exist a function \(g\in L^{1}[a, b]\) such that
\begin{equation} f(t)-f(a) =\int_{a}^{t} g(x)\, dx\tag{4.1} \end{equation}
holds?
in-context
